title: Get a Certificate of Competence activity code
body: "To work in a slaughterhouse that handles or slaughters animals for human consumption, you must have either a full Certificate of Competence (CoC) or a temporary Certificate of Competence.  Read Defra’s guidance on applying for a Certificate of Competence to slaughter or kill animals.  When you apply for a full or temporary CoC, you must provide an activity code for each type of animal and operation it will cover.  To get the activity codes:    Find the reference letter for the species, for example, ‘A’ for cattle.   Find the activity number for the operation in the tables, for example, ‘11’ for using a penetrative captive bolt device.   Combine the reference letter and activity number to create the activity code, for example, ‘A11’ for using a penetrative captive bolt device on cattle.    Red meat  The reference letter for the following species is:   A - for cattle, including water buffalo and bison  V - for calves  B - for horses, includes mules and donkeys  C - for sheep and goats  D - for pigs, including farmed wild boar  E - for large game, including feral wild boar, deer, alpacas, llamas and guanaco      Operation Activity number Use for Do not use for     Penetrative captive bolt device 11 All species (No restrictions)   Non-penetrative captive bolt device (simple stun only) 12 Calves less than 10kgSheep less than 10kgGoats less than 10kgLarge game less than 10kg CattleHorsesPigsHeavier calves, sheep, goats and large game   Non-penetrative captive bolt device (stun kill, England only) 12a Lambs less than 6kg Kids less than 4kg Piglets less than 10kg CalvesHorsesLarge gameHeavier sheep, goats and pigs   Firearm with free projectile 13 All species (No restrictions)   Pithing and the assessment of effective pithing 14 Cattle (not for human consumption) Calves (not for human consumption)Sheep and goats (not for human consumption)HorsesPigsLarge game (No restrictions)   Head-only electrical stunning 21 All species (No restrictions)   Head-to-body electrical stunning 22 All species (No restrictions)   Carbon dioxide at high concentration 31 Pigs only CattleCalvesHorsesSheep and goatsLarge game   Carbon dioxide associated with inert gases 33 Pigs only CattleCalvesHorsesSheep and goatsLarge game   Inert gases 34 Pigs only CattleCalvesHorsesSheep and goatsLarge game   The handling and care of animals before they are restrained 41 All species (No restrictions)   The restraint of animals for the purpose of stunning or killing 42 All species (No restrictions)   The shackling or hoisting of animals 43 All species (No restrictions)   The stunning of animals 51 All species (No restrictions)   The assessment of effective stunning 52 All species (No restrictions)   The bleeding of live animals, monitoring the absence of signs of life 53 All species (No restrictions)   Slaughtering in accordance with Article 4 (4) of Regulation EC 1099/2009 61 CattleCalvesSheep and goats HorsesPigsLarge game     Poultry, ratites and lagomorph  The reference letter for the following species is:   K - for chicken and guinea fowl  L - for quail  M - for turkey  N - for ratites, including ostrich, emu and rhea  Q - for ducks  P - for geese  R - for rabbits, including hares      Operation Activity number Use for Do not use for...
